statement: TSMC is ramping CoWoS advanced-packaging capacity (CAGR target above 80% for 2022-2027), narrowing the CoWoS supply-demand gap from roughly 20% earlier in 2026 to roughly 10% by end-2026.
why: Added CoWoS capacity eases the binding advanced-packaging bottleneck for AI accelerators, gradually improving accelerator supply even as demand stays strong.

impact.mechanism: CoWoS is the key advanced-packaging constraint for GPUs and HBM; capacity growth narrows the supply-demand gap and increases accelerator output.

evidence.excerpt: the CoWoS packaging supply-demand gap is estimated to narrow from roughly 20% earlier in 2026 to roughly 10% by end-2026, with a company CoWoS-capacity CAGR target 'above 80%' between 2022 and 2027.

confidence.basis: Secondary sources (Forbes with biyapay.com corroboration on guidance); two outlets, not filing-grade.
